title: storage-v3-out-of-tree: implement per-issue-ref storage layer
body:
# Goal

Implement v3 of jjforge's storage layer, moving every issue and
memory off the `issues` jj bookmark and onto per-issue git refs
under `refs/jjf/issues/<id>` and `refs/jjf/memories/<key>`.

End state:
- No `issues` bookmark exists post-migration.
- No mutating verb touches the jj working copy.
- `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` is gone; the source-repo guard is gone;
  the sibling-working-dir pattern in CLAUDE.md is gone.
- `jjf init` and every mutating verb works against any colocated
  jj+git repo without HEAD drift.
- `jjf push` / `jjf pull` round-trip via `refs/jjf/*:refs/jjf/*`,
  with remote-tracking refs at `refs/remotes/<remote>/jjf/*`.
- v2 â†’ v3 auto-migrates on first `Storage::open` of a v2-shape
  repo; the migration preserves the full op-log granularity per
  issue.

# Why now

`cc2fa96 host-asterinas-migrate` is blocked on this. The 4-CLI
jj write dance moves git HEAD onto `refs/jj/root` in any
colocated jj+git repo â€” the asterinas session lost the operator's
uncommitted work because of it. The redesign is the only
long-term fix; the `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` guard only protects
jjforge's own source tree, not any other host repo.

The design verdict is `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md` (landed
alongside this epic): per-issue refs, commit-chain-of-op-packs,
git-bug's pattern verified by the 34 archived `refs/bugs/*`
refs in this repo and the 421 v2 tests we have to port.

# Approach

Sketched child tickets (file as separate feature tickets under
this epic; each is roughly one session of work):

1. **Write path** â€” replace `Storage::try_commit_dance` with
   the git-only path (`git mktree` + `git commit-tree` +
   `git update-ref <ref> <new> <old>` for CAS). Every existing
   op writer routes through the new path. Preserve trailer
   serialization (`op.rs`) and boundary validation
   (`validate_title`, `validate_no_newlines`, self-dep check,
   phantom-target check, concurrent-write retry).

2. **Read path** â€” repoint `read_record`, `read_comments`,
   `list_records`, `replay_ops` at `refs/jjf/issues/<id>` and
   `refs/jjf/memories/<key>`. Repoint `history::read_history_at`
   to walk a per-issue ref's chain. Keep the debug-build
   op-replay cross-check.

3. **Init** â€” `Storage::init` writes `refs/jjf/meta/format-version`
   and exits. No bookmark creation.

4. **v2 â†’ v3 migrator** â€” mirror `maybe_migrate_v1_to_v2`'s
   shape. Walk the v2 op log per issue, replay each op-commit
   onto `refs/jjf/issues/<id>`, delete the `issues` bookmark
   when done. Idempotent via the `refs/jjf/meta/format-version`
   sentinel.

5. **Push / pull refspec + merge** â€” swap the refspec to
   `refs/jjf/*:refs/jjf/*` on push; on pull, fetch into
   `refs/remotes/<remote>/jjf/*` and run git-bug's five-scenario
   merge algorithm per-ref (no-op on identical, fast-forward on
   ancestor, merge-commit on divergence).

6. **Snapshot cache port** â€” recompute the cache key as the
   concatenation of all `refs/jjf/issues/*` and
   `refs/jjf/memories/*` ref shas. The cache structure on
   disk is unchanged.

7. **Test sweep** â€” port the 421-test workspace. CLI-surface
   tests (verb in / JSON out) should stay green without
   change; storage-internal tests need to assert the new ref
   layout instead of the bookmark layout.

8. **Delete the self-host guard + CLAUDE.md rewrite** â€” drop
   `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` and `refuse_self_hosted_write`. Rewrite
   the "Operating in a colocated jj+git repo" section of
   CLAUDE.md to describe v3's behavior. Drop the sibling-
   working-dir pattern. Issue `08cf14b` closes here too.

# Dispatch order

Serial, not parallel. Each child ticket mutates the storage
layer; concurrent dispatches would race. Suggested order:

1 â†’ 2 â†’ 3 â†’ 4 â†’ 5 â†’ 6 â†’ 7 â†’ 8

Tests should stay green between each child (run
`cargo nextest run --workspace` after each one lands). The
v2 â†’ v3 migrator (ticket 4) lands after read/write/init so
the migrator has a working v3 to migrate INTO.

# Scope

In scope:
- Everything listed above.

Out of scope:
- Changing the JSON schema. The on-disk shape (`Record`,
  `Comment`, `Memory`) stays the same.
- Changing the CLI surface. Every `jjf` verb behaves the
  same to the operator.
- Changing the trailer vocabulary. Op kinds unchanged.
- A v3 â†’ v2 downgrade path. Migration is one-shot; recovery
  is via a remote that hasn't been pushed-to yet.
- Web-UI visibility for issue data. Forgejo/Gitea/GitLab
  won't show `refs/jjf/*` in the web view â€” same as git-bug.

# Acceptance

- [ ] All 8 child tickets shipped, each with green tests.
- [ ] `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` removed from the codebase.
- [ ] `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs::try_commit_dance` removed.
- [ ] CLAUDE.md "Operating in a colocated jj+git repo" section
      rewritten or removed.
- [ ] `docs/storage-format.md` bumped to v3 with the per-issue-
      ref shape pinned.
- [ ] A fresh `jjf init` in an arbitrary colocated jj+git repo
      (e.g. `~/p/asterinas-workspace`) succeeds with no
      working-tree drift.
- [ ] `jjf push origin` / `jjf pull origin` round-trip via
      standard git transport without server-side config.

# Pointers

- Design doc: `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md`.
- Research ticket: `jjf show 487536a`.
- Blocked migration (unblocks when this epic ships):
  `jjf show cc2fa96`.
- Source guard issue (closes when ticket 8 lands):
  `jjf show 08cf14b`.
- Code to rewrite: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`,
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/read.rs`,
  `crates/jjf/src/preflight.rs`.
- Code to preserve: `crates/jjf-storage/src/trailer.rs`,
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/op.rs`,
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/history.rs`.
- Prior art: git-bug source (github.com/MichaelMure/git-bug)
  and the 34 archived `refs/bugs/*` refs in this repo.
